calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Sep-21-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Martha Bridegam for and with Paul Rose (Petitioner).

The Court stated that it read the memo regarding transmutation of property and asked if the cases cited involved separate property. Ms. Bridegam made reference to page 5 of the brief, to Family Codes 852(e) and 852(d), read case law, and spoke of co-mingling and amounts paid to maintain the properties for 34 years after the marriage. The Court asks if they could use apportioned ratios, and stated that they could take the value at the time of purchase and give the community share based on the pay-down of principal on the mortgage using the community property funds. Ms. Bridegam stated that they may not consider periods of loss especially with the Oakland property, that the Wife was an accountant, that she maintained separate account books and that separate account books stopped being maintained after the marriage intentionally. The Petition for Spousal Property is taken under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Aug-07-2017 Continued To Sep-06-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Laurie Shigekuni (by CourtCall) and Martha Bridgeman appearing for and with Murrell Paul Rose.

Ms. Shikeguni stated that there is a rebuttable presumption that the real property is community property. She informed the Court that Mr. Rose and decedent Pauline Mau were together as a couple beginning in 1973, but were not married until 1982. Two pieces of property were acquired as separate property during the time they were living together and prior to their marriage. She said that at the time of the marriage, there was an oral agreement between Mr. Rose and Ms. Mau that the properties were to be treated as community property, and all payments were made from community funds. Mr. Rose was sworn in as a witness, and stated that he and Miss Mau got together in 1973 with the understanding that the relationship was not necessarily permanent, but that they would be together as long as it was still fun. The first property acquired by them was located at 73 Madrid Street in San Francisco. Ms. Mau was working while Mr. Rose was attending school, so much of the money used initially for the purchase and mortgage came from her income. After Mr. Rose graduated, he had difficulty obtaining a job in San Francisco, and ultimately took a job in Washington DC. Ms. Mau decided it was still fun being with Mr. Rose, so she gave up her job and moved to Washington with him. Thereafter, Ms. Mau was unemployed, and Mr. Rose contributed all the monies used to pay for the Madrid Street property. After a couple of years, they moved back into the San Francisco property, and agreed that their contributions to the purchase of the property were equal. After that, the payments were made by them individually on alternate months, to keep their contributions equal. When their second property (in Oakland) was purchased in 1982 Mr. Rose put up 2/3 of the down payment so title was as tenants in common, 1/3 to Ms. Mau and 2/3 to Mr. Rose. After the parties were married in 1982, they no longer kept separate records of the payments for the properties. They maintained one checking account just for the Oakland property, which was a joint account. Because of bad tenants in the property at the time it was acquired, the Oakland property was in the red for the first 3-4 years, but after that generated profits up to $2000 per month. The income from the profits was used by the couple for their joint activities.
The Court requested Counsel to provide further briefing on the issue of comingling of funds.
Ms. Shigekuni agreed to sumbit supplemental papers on the issue by August 30, 2017. The matter was continued to September 6, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for further hearing.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Nuque. (Not reported)
